# Run Report: only-lvmt-s07 — LVMT 1M Stat Benchmark (64 Shards)

**Experiment ID:** only-lvmt-s07  
**Algorithm:** LVMT  
**Key count:** 1,000,000 (1m)  
**Variant:** stat (read-only), 64 shards  
**Date:** 2026-04-15  
**Status:** COMPLETE

---

## Environment

| Parameter       | Value                           |
|-----------------|---------------------------------|
| Machine         | fcs-0033                        |
| OS              | Linux (cgroup v2)               |
| Rust toolchain  | 1.67.0                          |
| Memory limit    | 8 GB (cgroup `/sys/fs/cgroup/lvmt`) |
| `drop_caches`   | 3 (before benchmark start)      |
| `CXXFLAGS`      | `-Wno-error -include cstdint`   |

## Command

```bash
cargo run --release -- --stat --warmup-from ./warmup/v4 -k 1m -a lvmt --shards 64
```

## Results Summary

| Metric              | Value         |
|---------------------|---------------|
| Epochs completed    | 198           |
| Total time          | 141.580 s     |
| Throughput          | 140,196 ops/s |
| Latency (avg)       | 7.133 µs/op   |
| Empty reads         | 0             |
| Read amplification  | 1.144         |
| Write amplification | 1.144         |
| Avg levels          | 1.673         |
| Access writes       | [1003, 15839, 0, 2] |
| Data writes         | 200,000       |

## Key Observations

1. **16% slower than plain.** 64-shard overhead adds ~1.2 µs/op for shard routing
   and access metadata lookups.
2. **Read/Write amp = 1.144.** Each operation touches ~1.14 nodes on average due to
   shard boundary crossings.
3. **Access writes: 1003 + 15839.** Even in stat (read-only) mode, 64-shard LVMT
   writes ~17k access metadata entries per run — shard routing tables are updated
   on read.
4. **141.6s vs 140.0s (time benchmark).** Stat and time benchmarks are nearly
   identical, confirming negligible stat overhead.
